data.description:
## Why
Collapse the reviewer-centric model into the unified prompt model: reviewers become a SUBSET of
prompts; the catalog becomes a generated derived index. Validated by spike E5.

## What
- Migrate the 6 reviewers' `catalog.json` metadata into per-file front-matter using S2's writer
  (`src/rebar/llm/reviewers/*.md`).
- Generalize `get_reviewer` -> `resolve_prompt` in `src/rebar/llm/prompts.py`: resolution
  project `.rebar/prompts/<id>.md` override > built-in packaged; reviewer membership EXPLICIT
  (`category: review` / reviewer flag), NEVER inferred from output-schema shape.
- Build the GENERATED + COMMITTED derived-index (scan front-matter -> index) enforcing invariants
  (exactly-one default; no `dimension` collision; preserve `langfuse_prompt`/`fallback_file`/
  `applies_to`). Add a CI drift gate (`regenerate + git diff --exit-code`) in
  `.github/workflows/test.yml`. `applies_to` keeps stdlib `fnmatch`.

## Scope
In: migration + resolver + index + CI gate + regression-equivalence. Out: prompt CONTRACTS in the
runner (S4), editor library (S6).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] The 6 reviewers carry front-matter; `resolve_prompt` resolves project>built-in; reviewer flag explicit.
- [ ] Derived index generated+committed; invariants enforced (two-default AND dimension-collision rejections both tested); CI drift gate fails on a stale index.
- [ ] Existing reviewer behavior preserved (resolution/default/applies_to routing) — equivalence regression tests green.
data.title: Reviewer->prompt migration + resolver generalization + derived-index lockfile
